title: Minnesota Sues Trump Administration for Withholding Evidence in Three ICE Shootings
excerpt: Minnesota, Hennepin County, and the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension sued the Trump administration to compel access to evidence in the three Minneapolis-area ICE shootings, alleging the federal government 'reneged on its promise' to cooperate with state investigations and had adopted 'a policy of categorically withholding evidence.'
content:
On March 24, 2026, the state of Minnesota, Hennepin County Attorney's Office, and the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension filed suit in federal court against the U.S. Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security, seeking court-ordered access to evidence in three Minneapolis ICE shootings.

  • The lawsuit covered all three shooting incidents during Operation Metro Surge: the deaths of Renee Good (January 7) and Alex Pretti (January 24), and the non-fatal shooting of Venezuelan national Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis (January 14).
  • Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty stated the federal government had 'adopted a policy of categorically withholding evidence,' calling the practice 'unprecedented and alarming.' State officials said federal agents first agreed to cooperate, then took control of evidence and denied access.
  • At the Pretti shooting scene, federal agents physically blocked BCA investigators from accessing the area. Evidence the state was denied included Pretti's cell phone, Good's car (described as shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse and never examined), and personnel files for the agents involved.
  • The lawsuit argued the federal government 'is not permitted to withhold investigative evidence for the purpose of shielding law enforcement officers from scrutiny where a State is investigating serious potential violations of its criminal laws.'
  • State investigators specifically sought training records for ICE agent Jonathan Ross, who shot Good—including when he was trained and what he was trained to do.
'For instance, Jonathan Ross, when was he trained? What was he trained to do? What's in his personnel file? That's true of every agent that was involved in these shootings.' — Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty
mediaPlaceholder: Minnesota state lawsuit filing against DOJ and DHS demanding access to evidence from three ICE shooting scenes
